Permutation_properties.solution: (* Manual grade exercise - no code solution needed *)
Permutation_properties.explanation: This is a manually graded exercise asking students to list desirable properties of Permutation. Examples: 5. Permutation preserves length. 6. Permutation is transitive. 7. If Permutation al bl, then for any x, count x al = count x bl.

permut_example.solution:
Proof.
  intros.
  rewrite app_nil_r.
  apply perm_skip.
  change (6 :: a ++ b) with ((6 :: a) ++ b).
  change (b ++ 6 :: a) with (b ++ (6 :: a)).
  apply Permutation_app_comm.
Qed.
permut_example.explanation: We simplify the nil at the end of the right side, then use perm_skip for 5, and Permutation_app_comm to swap (6::a) and b.

not_a_permutation.solution:
Proof.
  intro H.
  apply Permutation_cons_inv in H.
  apply Permutation_length_1_inv in H.
  discriminate.
Qed.
not_a_permutation.explanation: We remove the common head element 1 using Permutation_cons_inv, then use Permutation_length_1_inv on the remaining singleton lists [1] and [2], which yields 1=2, a contradiction.

Forall_perm.solution:
Proof.
  intros A f al bl Hperm Hforall.
  induction Hperm.
  - assumption.
  - inv Hforall. constructor; auto.
  - inv Hforall. inv H2. constructor; auto. constructor; auto.
  - auto.
Qed.
Forall_perm.explanation: By induction on the permutation evidence. The skip case uses inversion on Forall; the swap case inverts twice; the trans case chains the two inductive hypotheses.
